name: Japanese Novel
comment: Instant (pre-activation) colorization for .jpnov. Scope families are chosen so VS Code's DEFAULT semantic-type→scope fallback lands on the SAME colors the LSP semantic layer later emits (comment→comment, keyword→keyword — see src/server/semanticTokens.ts HIGHLIGHTS): when the server's tokens arrive they refine invisibly instead of repainting. Every pattern is a single-line `match` (never begin/end), and the tokenizer bounds delimiter pairing to the line too, so both layers agree: an unclosed ［＃ is DELIBERATELY greyed to its line end (the semantic layer marks the same span as `marker` and the server flags it as an Error), while a lone 《 or a stray ］/》 stays default text. The 傍点/傍線/太字/斜体 variant alternations and the single-line ［＃○字下げ］ line-head (^) anchor are checked against src/shared/compiler/emphasis.ts by test/shared/compiler/grammar-sync.test.ts (canonical length-desc order) — regenerate an alternation from that test's failure output, never by hand. ［＃○字下げ］ takes full-width digits only ([０-９]+, per the locked spec); a mid-line one is inert and deliberately falls through to the generic comment rule (the server tokenizer degrades it to a comment too, so both layers grey it).

patterns.comment: Page break ［＃改ページ］ — frame greyed, directive keyword-colored (semantic: marker/directive).

Block END ［＃ここで(字下げ|太字|斜体)終わり］ — whole inner is one directive (mirrors the semantic layer's markDirective). BEFORE block START (終わり suffix) and BEFORE the span rules. ここで傍点終わり etc. is NOT here (傍点/傍線 have no block form) and falls through to the generic comment rule, exactly like the tokenizer.
patterns.match: (［＃)(ここで(?:字下げ|太字|斜体)終わり)(］)

patterns.comment: Block START ［＃ここから(○字下げ|太字|斜体)］ (○ = full-width digits ONLY). The hanging-indent 折り返して form has extra text before ］ so it falls through to the generic comment rule, exactly like the tokenizer.

Single-line ［＃○字下げ］ — LINE-HEAD ONLY (^). A mid-line one is inert (the server tokenizer degrades it to a comment) → it must fall through to the generic comment rule and stay grey in BOTH layers. Full-width digits only.
傍点/傍線 span END ［＃(の左に|左に)?<dot|line>終わり］ — must be tried BEFORE span start (終わり suffix).
patterns.match: (［＃)(の左に|左に)?(二重丸傍点|白ゴマ傍点|白三角傍点|蛇の目傍点|黒三角傍点|ばつ傍点|二重傍線|白丸傍点|×傍点|丸傍点|傍点|傍線|波線|破線|鎖線)(終わり)(］)

patterns.comment: 傍点/傍線 span START ［＃(の左に|左に)?<dot|line>］.
patterns.match: (［＃)(の左に|左に)?(二重丸傍点|白ゴマ傍点|白三角傍点|蛇の目傍点|黒三角傍点|ばつ傍点|二重傍線|白丸傍点|×傍点|丸傍点|傍点|傍線|波線|破線|鎖線)(］)

patterns.comment: 太字/斜体 inline span END ［＃(太字|斜体)終わり］ — before its span START (終わり suffix).
太字/斜体 inline span START ［＃(太字|斜体)］.

傍点/傍線 postfix ［＃「対象」(に)?(の左に|左に)?<dot|line>］ — connector is に ONLY (の is NOT a connector; の左に is the direction prefix, so a lone の+variant falls through to the generic comment rule, exactly like the tokenizer). The target (group 3) is deliberately UNSCOPED so it keeps the default foreground, exactly like the semantic layer. The target must be NON-empty ([^」]+): the tokenizer rejects an empty target (comment fallback), so an empty one must fall through to the generic annotation rule below.
patterns.match: (［＃)(「)([^」]+)(」)(に)?(の左に|左に)?(二重丸傍点|白ゴマ傍点|白三角傍点|蛇の目傍点|黒三角傍点|ばつ傍点|二重傍線|白丸傍点|×傍点|丸傍点|傍点|傍線|波線|破線|鎖線)(］)

patterns.comment: 太字/斜体 postfix ［＃「対象」は(太字|斜体)］ — connector は is REQUIRED (not に), no direction. A mis-paired connector (は+傍点, に+太字) matches neither postfix rule and falls through to the generic comment rule, exactly like the tokenizer's connector matrix.

Any other ［＃…］ (block layout, 見出し, the 折り返して indent form, a mid-line 字下げ, a half-width digit, a mis-paired connector, unknown) — whole annotation greyed, matching the server's `comment` classification. Single-line, so an unclosed ［＃ never runs away.
patterns.name: comment.block.annotation.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Unterminated ［＃… with no ］ before the line end — greyed exactly like a closed annotation (the tokenizer swallows the same span as a `brokenAnnotation` and the semantic layer marks it `marker`; the server adds the Error squiggle). $ bounds it to the line; LAST so every closed rule above wins first ([^］]*$ never matches once a closing ］ follows the opener on the line).
patterns.name: comment.block.annotation.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Ruby reading 《…》 incl. both brackets — greyed like the semantic marker; the base (親文字) before it keeps the default foreground. Empty 《》 / a lone 《 are left default (the tokenizer treats them literally).
patterns.name: comment.other.ruby.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Explicit ruby base marker ｜ — the 親文字 after it stays default.
patterns.name: comment.other.ruby.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Dialogue corner-bracket delimiters — greyed; the utterance content keeps the default foreground (the semantic layer masks it too). Matching per CHARACTER keeps multi-line 「…」 stateless and safe.
patterns.name: comment.other.dialogue.novel-jp
